description: The canonical pipeline for .context-managed work — THE required shape, no triviality exemption and no ad-hoc path. In order: (1) plan mode -> EXPLORE by dispatching Explore/eval agents (read-only, against current source, never from memory, never assume docs match runtime) -> write plan to ~/.claude/plans/ encoding steps 2-5; (2) PRE-IMPL file .context task items (+ story/feature/phase membership + depends_on/gated_by edges) BEFORE launching IMPL; (3) IMPL agent(s) (self-contained directive; STOP on ambiguity); (4) a SEPARATE agent (not the implementer) adversarially audits the IMPL output; (5) POST-IMPL status cascade (task status + verification_verifies_item edge + context-validate; cascade task->story->feature->phase) + orchestrator-independent gate re-run. ITERATE TO ZERO: any finding from the adversarial audit, the gate, or context-validate -> loop re-explore/eval -> re-plan -> re-IMPL -> re-adversarial-audit until clean. The fix loop EXPLORE precedes its plan exactly as initially — a finding is investigated by Explore/eval agents to full root-cause context before any fix plan is written, NOT planned from the orchestrator read of the finding alone. Work is not done while any finding stands; a nonzero audit is never accepted. Authoritative operative copy in CLAUDE.md Active-phase-management; .context is this phase focused status surface while the decomposed-JSON session-start pointer still holds.
enforcement: manual; context-validate machine-verifies the task->story->feature->phase chain in step 4
